heading: Special rules for disability retirement and death-in-service benefits with respect to certain employees
label: 3519 Special rules for disability retirement and death-in-service benefits with respect to certain employees

(2) which read as follows: “The annuities and/or other benefits provided under subsections (c) and (d) of this section shall be deemed to be annuities and/or benefits under chapter 84 of title 5 for purposes of the other provisions of such chapter and other laws (including title 26) relating to such annuities and/or benefits, but shall be payable from the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability Fund established by section 202 of the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement Act of 1964 for Certain Employees.”Pub. children.heading: Officers and employees to whom section 2051 rules apply
children.label: a Officers and employees to whom section 2051 rules apply

children.text: has five years of civilian service credit toward retirement under such subchapter III of chapter 83, title 5;
has not been designated under as a participant in the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability System;section 2013 of this title
has become disabled during a period of assignment to the performance of duties that are qualifying toward such designation under such ; andsection 2013 of this title
satisfies the requirements for disability retirement under —section 8337 of title 5

children.heading: Survivors of officers and employees to whom section 2052 rules apply
children.label: b Survivors of officers and employees to whom section 2052 rules apply

children.text: has at least eighteen months of civilian service credit toward retirement under such subchapter III of chapter 83, title 5;
has not been designated under , as a participant in the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability System;section 2013 of this title1So in original. The comma probably should not appear.1

prior to separation or retirement from the Agency, dies during a period of assignment to the performance of duties that are qualifying toward such designation under such ; andsection 2013 of this title
is survived by a surviving spouse, former spouse, or child as defined in , who would otherwise be entitled to an annuity under —section 2002 of this titlesection 8341 of title 5

children.heading: Annuities under this section deemed annuities under chapter 83 of title 5
children.label: c Annuities under this section deemed annuities under chapter 83 of title 5
children.text: The annuities provided under subsections (a) and (b) of this section shall be deemed to be annuities under chapter 83 of title 5 for purposes of the other provisions of such chapter and other laws (including title 26) relating to such annuities, and shall be payable from the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability Fund maintained pursuant to .section 2012 of this title
